steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $6{x}^{2}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Both terms share the factor $x^2$. Subtract the coefficients: $13x^2 - 7x^2 = (13 - 7)x^2 = 6x^2$.

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-91{x}^{2}$: $-91$ would come from multiplying $13 \times (-7)$, not subtracting. ✗
  • Choice B — $6{x}^{2}$: $13 - 7 = 6$, so this is correct. ✓
  • Choice C — $20{x}^{2}$: $20$ would come from adding $13 + 7$, not subtracting. ✗
  • Choice D — $40{x}^{2}$: No arithmetic operation on 13 and 7 yields 40. ✗
That matches Choice B — $6{x}^{2}$.
